commands.purpose: Enumerate registry rows. --kind=sport|discipline|position|all (default all). Filters by tenant / category / platform-vs-custom / active. Prints a table with id, tenant (or 'platform'), kind, name, slug, category (Sport only), parent slug (Discipline/Position), is_active, downstream_reference_count. Ops tooling.

Print full detail for one row. --kind=sport|discipline|position (default sport). Shows the row + optional children (disciplines under a sport; positions under a discipline) + optional downstream reference counts across every table in the reconciler config. Useful for debugging why a downstream Wave 3 row's sport_key doesn't resolve.

Call the SportKeyResolver + print the full resolution trace. Useful for debugging why a downstream row's sport_key fails to resolve. Prints the candidates checked (tenant custom active → platform active → tenant custom historical → platform historical) + which layer matched (or that no layer matched).

Dispatch SeedPlatformSportsCatalogJob. Idempotent — a no-op when the catalog is already seeded unless --force. With --force, UPSERTS every row from `data/platform-sports-catalog.json` (adds new, updates existing) + deactivates rows removed from the JSON. --dry-run reports the planned adds/updates/deactivates without persisting. Refused when the job is already running (RESEED_LOCKED_IN_USE).
Alias for `sports:registry:seed-platform-catalog --force`. Explicit re-seed command for platform ops running after a JSON edit. --dry-run reports the planned changes.

Force-run the ReconcileSportReferencesJob. --tenant scopes to a single tenant; without it, scans every tenant. --table scopes to a single reference table (e.g. `teams`); without it, sweeps every table in `config('sports_registry.reconciler.reference_tables')`. --dry-run reports the drift without firing SportPrefixResolutionFailed events (so callers can preview without triggering notifications).

CLI shortcut for POST /api/v1/sports. Requires the tenant to hold `sports_registry_custom` entitlement (Enterprise-only). Refuses when slug collides with a platform slug or an existing tenant slug. Consumes 1 sports_registry_custom_slot.

CLI shortcut for POST /api/v1/disciplines. Requires the tenant to hold `sports_registry_custom` entitlement. Parent Sport (identified by slug) must be either platform-seeded OR the tenant's own custom. Cross-tenant parent refused with CROSS_TENANT_REGISTRY_REFERENCE.
CLI shortcut for POST /api/v1/positions. Same tenant + parent validation as create-custom-discipline.

Deactivate a Sport / Discipline / Position by slug. --kind selects the entity type (default sport). --cascade sweeps active children (Sport → deactivate all Disciplines + Positions; Discipline → deactivate all Positions). --force-hard-delete bypasses the retention window + hard-deletes an already-deactivated custom row (requires ops sign-off; never applies to platform-seeded rows). Refuses when downstream references exist (SPORT_REFERENCE_IN_USE / etc.).
